stem: A education study measures average test score improvement across 3 schools, each containing multiple classrooms. school A has 10 observations with mean 74.9 points (within-school SD = 10.0). school B has 10 observations with mean 85.2 points (within-school SD = 6.8). school C has 5 observations with mean 63.9 points (within-school SD = 5.3). The between-school standard deviation is uncertain, estimated between 6.5 and 16.4 points. Using a hierarchical Bayesian model, estimate the true average test score improvement for school A, accounting for shrinkage toward the grand mean and the uncertainty in the between-school variation.

ground_truth.answer: Hierarchical estimate for school A: 75.0 points (shrunk from raw 74.9). Range [75.0, 75.3] as between-school SD varies from 6.5 to 16.4. Pooled mean: 76.8.

solution_steps: Compute pooled (grand) mean: 76.8 points (weighted average of 3 school means).
Shrinkage factor for school A: B = σ²_between / (σ²_between + σ²_within/10). At σ_between=10.9: B = 0.922.
Interval [75.0, 75.3] captures uncertainty from the unknown between-group variance.
common_failure_modes: Using the raw observed mean (74.9) without shrinkage
Using only the grand mean (76.8) and ignoring group-specific data
Not recognizing that small-sample groups should be shrunk more toward the grand mean
Treating the between-group variance as known rather than uncertain
